chunk_summary: Guidelines for managing pneumonia with hypoxia in children with severe acute malnutrition (SAM), including oxygen therapy, empiric antibiotics, and step-down criteria for transitioning to oral agents.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical signs and management of pneumonia with hypoxia in SAM.
Learn the appropriate use of empiric antibiotics and step-down criteria in pneumonia treatment.
Recognize the importance of monitoring oxygen saturation and vital signs in SAM-related pneumonia.
key_facts: Suspect severe pneumonia in SAM when oxygen saturation is <90% (<94% if pneumonia is present).
First-line empiric antibiotics: injectable ampicillin plus gentamicin; add cloxacillin for suspected staphylococcal pneumonia.
Step down to oral antibiotics when respiratory distress resolves, oxygen is minimal, and oral feeds are tolerated.
Complete a 5–7 day antibiotic course for uncomplicated severe pneumonia; extend for complications or staphylococcal pneumonia.
Blood cultures and chest radiographs are recommended for very ill children or when complications are suspected.
reference_quote: Empiric antibiotics for admitted/complicated SAM should align to one local standard. A common first-line regimen is injectable ampicillin plus gentamicin.
